white side and now this is probably going to

be more more useful for the people that are going 
into the intermediate phase of chess understanding

not just the beginners chess has two types of 
openings setup based openings and theory-based

openings a setup-based opening is an opening that 
you can basically play the same way all the time

an example is the queen's pawn london opening and 
regardless of how black plays you go like this

you play knight f3 bishop f4 on your next move 
then you play e3 then you put this knight here

so for example knight d2 then the bishop here 
and then you complete this little pawn pyramid

and then you castle it's called a setup based 
opening because you set it up every single game

now this is one way another opening with the 
white pieces that's setup based uh there's a lot

of queen spawn ones that are set up based like the 
kali for example another one is the english which

is when you play c4 and then on your next move 
you play g3 and bishop g2 so something like this

and you fight for the center in the following way 
with this knight and then this knight likes to

come here you play e3 knight e2 and castle and 
again you can play the setup against basically

anything um those are good openings for folks 
that don't want to learn a whole lot of theory

theory based openings for example e4 is a theory 
based opening what is that a theory based opening

is when you need to know specific responses that 
are called that are considered the best against

what your opponent is playing when you play the 
king's pawn you need to know how to deal with

the king's pawn response the sicilian the karo 
khan the french and there's many many others those

are the four main ones but against the king's 
pawn black can play a whole variety of things

and so those you might need a book you might need

uh you know literally anything so the most

indian setup and it goes like this d6 
knight f6 g6 to put the bishop here

and then castles then you develop the queen
